stories.headline: Trump says he is cancelling fresh strikes on Iran and hints at possible peace deal
stories.summary: Trump’s social media post comes after the US and Iran traded strikes for a third day, pushing the Middle East closer to the resumption of a full-scale war.

stories.headline: Israel deports French journalist accused of supporting Hamas

stories.headline: Trump Cancels Planned Iran Strikes in Latest Reversal
stories.summary: Jonathan Panikoff, director of the Scowcroft Middle East Security Initiative at the Atlantic Council, said that there is a chance that President Trump was pushing for something that Iranian negotiators had not 'fully signed off on' when he announced the cancellation of planned strikes on Iran. Panikoff said that despite the quickly changing narrative over a few hours, it is more likely if a deal comes together it is because of ongoing negotiation

stories.headline: India protests to US after three sailors killed in tanker attack
stories.summary: New Delhi calls for ‘de-escalation’ in Gulf but appears to have little appetite for confrontation with Washington

stories.headline: Trump names former SEC head Jay Clayton as US intelligence chief
stories.summary: President has faced backlash in Congress over his pick of Bill Pulte as acting director of national intelligence

stories.headline: US Stocks Soar as Trump Signals US-Iran Peace Deal Approaching
stories.summary: US stocks soared to their best day since April, halting a two-day slide, as President Donald Trump said he’s called off planned military strikes on Iran, hours after threatening to escalate the three-month war.

stories.headline: Trump Says Deal With Iran Could Be Signed Within Days
stories.summary: President Donald Trump says a deal to end the war with Iran could be signed within days. “We just made a great settlement of the war with Iran, and we’re going to be subject to finalization of documents, we should get done over the next few days,” Trump said. Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld report.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Property deeds no protection for Palestinians as settler violence spreads
stories.summary: Israeli settlers follow Palestinian Bedouins across the West Bank, chasing them from their land.

stories.headline: Avril Haines, Former Intelligence Chief, Will Lead Carnegie Endowment for International Peace
stories.summary: In the run-up to the invasion of Ukraine, Ms. Haines helped devise the strategy of declassifying evidence of Russia’s military buildup in an effort to rally European allies.

stories.headline: Thai court sentences two Uyghur men to death for 2015 Bangkok bombing
stories.summary: Twenty people were killed and 120 injured in the attack at the Erawan Shrine, a popular tourist destinationA Thai court has handed out death sentences to two Uyghur men from the north-western Chinese region of Xinjiang for a 2015 bombing in the centre of Bangkok that killed 20 people.The explosion occurred at the Erawan Shrine in the centre of Bangkok, an area popular with foreign tourists. As well as the 20 people killed, another 120 were injure

stories.headline: Trump to pick US attorney Jay Clayton for director of national intelligence
stories.summary: Nomination comes amid pushback over Donald Trump's selection of Bill Pulte as acting director of the agency.

stories.headline: US slaps sanctions on Cuba's oil and gas company, accusing it of weaponising energy
stories.summary: Cuba is already struggling under a decades-old embargo and a lack of petroleum as the US keeps pushing for a change in its economic and political model.

stories.headline: Russia 'lost standing’ despite ‘a breather’ from higher oil prices, IMF chief says
stories.summary: While Russia has emerged as one of the very few winners from the recent disruptions to the global oil market amid the Iran war, this is not enough to offset the hit of sanctions and isolation, the managing director of the International Monetary Fund (IMF) told Euronews.

stories.headline: IMF warns against further relaxation of euro area fiscal rules
stories.summary: The IMF has urged euro area countries to tighten their fiscal policies rather than relaxing them further in response to the energy crisis, with an extra adjustment effort required from high-debt nations.

stories.headline: US and Iran exchange strikes across Middle East for second day in a row
stories.summary: Iran says it responded to US strikes by targeting American assets in Kuwait, Jordan and Bahrain, which reported damage to its capital city overnight.

stories.headline: US Bonds Surge as Trump’s Vow That War Is Ending Sends Oil Lower
stories.summary: Treasuries rallied after comments by US President Donald Trump on the war against Iran sparked a slide in oil prices that caused traders to price in lower chances of a Federal Reserve interest-rate hike this year.

stories.headline: Bloomberg Tech 2026 Opening Video
stories.summary: Bloomberg Tech 2026 took place in San Francisco bringing together the leading CEOs, investors and innovators who are harnessing technology to change the world around us. The summit opened with a look at the state of the industry and AI.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Vance Boelter Pleads Guilty in Assassination of Minnesota Lawmaker
stories.summary: Federal prosecutors said they would not seek the death penalty for Vance Boelter, who still faces charges in state court.

stories.headline: Justice Dept. Pushes Limits of Its Power Over State Elections
stories.summary: The department once tried to stay out of state elections, urging caution. It is now pressing forward with claims of fraud as President Trump revives his unfounded assertions that elections cannot be trusted.

stories.headline: French singer Patrick Bruel under formal investigation for rape

stories.headline: New Zealand Two-Speed Growth Gets Boost From Farms as Cities Lag
stories.summary: In 30 years working in New Zealand’s agriculture sector, Mark Leslie can’t recall a sweeter spot of strong commodity prices and cooperative weather.

Cyprus proposes €32.8bn cut to next EU budget as compromise between rival camps
stories.summary: Cyprus proposes a 2% cut — worth €32.8bn — to the EU's draft €2 trillion budget for 2028–2034, in a bid to bridge the gap between "frugals" and cohesion-minded member states. The figures, now on the table for the first time, will serve as the starting point for the sensitive negotiations ahead.

stories.summary: Oil plunged to the lowest since April as US President Donald Trump said that a peace deal with Iran could be signed as soon as the weekend, fueling bets for an end to the war that’s disrupted global energy shipments.

stories.headline: US strikes Iran in response to downing of military helicopter
stories.summary: President Donald Trump earlier accused Iran of shooting down the US helicopter over the Strait of Hormuz and vowed to respond.

stories.headline: Inside Myanmar, rebels are losing ground as military forces men into army

stories.headline: Man shot dead during protest against proposed US Ebola quarantine facility in Kenya

Reporters from the agency saw his body lying motionless in a police van with a large head wound.

stories.headline: Russia's fuel crisis intensifies as Ukraine steps up strikes on occupied territories

stories.headline: 'No dead ends': What the Dutch can teach us about tackling youth unemployment
